fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maine Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maine - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity maine, how to transfer pharmacist license to maine, pharmacist compact maine, maine pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Maine Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Maine Office of Professional and Occupational Regulation (OPOR)
board.mailing_address:
Board of Pharmacy
35 State House Station
Augusta, ME 04333-0035

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Maine.

steps.title: Create Account & Submit Application
steps.description: Register as a first-time user on the Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Licensing System and complete the online application for Pharmacist by Examination.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your ACPE-accredited college of pharmacy and documentation of 1500 internship hours (e.g., Maine Board of Pharmacy internship log or NABP Internship Equivalency Form) to be sent directly to the Maine Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Register for NABP Exams
steps.description: Register and pay for both the NAPLEX and Maine Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) with the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP).

steps.title: Pass Licensing Exams
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on both the NAPLEX and the Maine MPJE. Your license will be issued upon successful completion and review of all application requirements by the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Maine pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Maine have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Maine. To work in Maine, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Maine?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maine while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Maine
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Maine, you must register and submit your application online through the Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Licensing System.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.portal_name: Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Licensing System
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Licensing System. If you are a first-time user, click 'Register here' or 'First-Time User Registration' to create an account by providing your personal information, email, and setting up a password and security questions. Once registered, log in to access and complete the appropriate pharmacist application.
